channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Family Opposes Autopsy on Physiotherapist Found Dead in Umahi's Residence
item.description: The Ebonyi State Police Command revealed that the family of Mary Habila, a physiotherapist found dead in the residence of Minister David Umahi, has refused to allow an autopsy. Despite the family's op...

item.title: Ace Banzuelo Returns to Rappler Live Jam with New Singles
item.description: Ace Banzuelo, a self-taught artist who began singing at age four, is set to return to Rappler Live Jam on July 16, 2026. Known for tracks like 'Muli,' 'Himala,' and 'Malayo,' Banzuelo has been explori...

item.title: HER Initiative Launches Panda Academy to Empower 50 Young Women in Beauty Economy
item.description: HER Initiative, a Tanzanian women's empowerment organization, has announced the selection of 50 young women for its Panda Academy programme, a six-month training initiative aimed at transforming their...

item.title: Trump Faces Risks of Prolonged Conflict in Iran Amid Diplomatic Deadlock
item.description: President Donald Trump's potential involvement in a prolonged conflict with Iran has raised concerns about repeating historical patterns of 'forever wars'. Despite his campaign promises to end wars, t...

item.title: AFRIMMA 2023 Winners: Recognizing Excellence in African Music and Entertainment
item.description: The article lists the winners of various categories at the AFRIMMA (Africa Music Awards) event, highlighting top talents across music, dance, and entertainment. Categories include Best DJ Africa, Best...

item.title: ED probes money laundering network linked to illegal immigration and forged documents
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted raids at 13 locations across Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Haryana, and West Bengal as part of a money laundering investigation. The probe targets a syndicate allege...

item.title: Wildfire smoke persists in GTA with Environment Canada issuing continued air quality warnings
item.description: A thick, smokey haze continues to affect the Greater Toronto Area (GTA) as Environment Canada maintains an orange-level air quality warning. The air quality health index is at 10+ or 'very high risk' ...

item.title: Guenka FM Presenter's Journey from Sales Struggles to Radio Success
item.description: Guenka FM presenter Njenga has shared her challenging journey from struggling in sales and marketing to becoming a respected radio presenter. She faced constant rejections and self-doubt in her early ...

item.title: Argentina stun England with late comeback to reach FIFA World Cup 2026 final
item.description: In a thrilling semi-final clash at the FIFA World Cup 2026, Argentina delivered a stunning late comeback to defeat England and secure their place in the final. England had taken an early lead through ...

item.title: Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Linked to Contaminated Lettuce Spreads Across the U.S.
item.description: The United States is facing a significant cyclosporiasis outbreak, with nearly 7,000 potential cases reported, over 3,300 in Michigan alone. The parasite, which causes severe gastrointestinal distress...

item.title: EU Court Upholds Spain's Amnesty Law for Catalan Separatists
item.description: The European Union's top court has ruled in favor of Spain's 2024 amnesty law for Catalan separatists, clearing the way for Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's political strategy. The law, passed to secure...

item.title: Tragedy in Kiambu: Twin Brother Killed, Sibling Injured in Road Grader Accident
item.description: A tragic road accident in Kiambu county has left a family in mourning after a road grader struck two twin brothers on their way home from school. The incident occurred on Kamandura–Rironi Road, part o...

item.title: Delta State Police Arrest Content Creator Over Rape Allegations
item.description: The Delta State Police Command has arrested Ifeanyi Ogbonna, known as 'Odogwu of Asaba,' over allegations that he raped a 20-year-old woman who later died. The arrest followed a petition stating the w...

item.title: Media's Use of 'Trial of the Century' Label in Duterte Impeachment Case
item.description: The article examines how media outlets have framed Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial as a 'trial of the century,' comparing it to past high-profile cases. It highlights the tendency of j...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Warns Regional Tensions Could Impact Pakistan's Economy
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has cautioned that escalating tensions in the Middle East, particularly the recent US-Iran clashes, could negatively affect Pakistan's economy. During a meeting to assess...

item.title: Creating a Home Spa: Simple Tips for Relaxation
item.description: The article explores how to transform your bathroom into a personal spa sanctuary for relaxation and self-care. Author Chantel Mila emphasizes that creating a spa-like atmosphere at home doesn't requi...

item.title: Senior Traditional Leader Nkosi Mpumalanga Gwadiso Dies Suddenly in Eastern Cape
item.description: Nkosi Mpumalanga Gwadiso, 63, the chair of the Eastern Cape House of Traditional and Khoisan Leaders, died suddenly in his sleep at his home in Ngqeleni. His death was confirmed by family members and ...

item.title: UK government rejects mandatory breathalysers in cars, prioritises other safety measures
item.description: The UK government has decided not to make breathalysers (alcolocks) mandatory in new cars, despite considering them as a potential tool to combat drink-driving. Following a consultation that closed in...

item.title: Crowd congestion during Puri Rath Yatra leads to multiple cases of fainting among devotees
item.description: During the annual Puri Rath Yatra festival, a significant number of devotees experienced health issues due to overcrowding and lack of proper crowd management. Reports indicate that several individual...

item.title: Advocates Push for Safer Drug Supply to Combat Overdose Crisis in Manitoba
item.description: The article highlights the growing concern over opioid-related deaths in Manitoba, where advocates are urging policymakers to consider safer drug supply programs as a potential solution. Families of t...
